description: One-document narrative-fiction and known-prose containment thermometer for Prethinker using Arthur Conan Doyle's The Red-Headed League.
purpose: Test whether audit grammar can extract narrative facts from prose while staying source-bound when the underlying model may already know the famous public-domain story.
score_interpretation: Mechanism probe for narrative reasoning and prior-knowledge containment; not clean unseen transfer evidence.

file_structure.holmes_001_red_headed_league/source_orig: Raw extracted Gutenberg story text, Adventure II only
file_structure.holmes_001_red_headed_league/source.md: Faithful Markdown rendering of Adventure II only
file_structure.holmes_001_red_headed_league/oracle.json: 25 answer rows with source spans and difficulty
Source, extraction, copyright, and span notes

data_discipline.source_authenticity: Project Gutenberg public-domain text; Adventure II only
data_discipline.extraction_fidelity: HTML to Markdown while preserving paragraph order, dialogue, punctuation, and italic spans
data_discipline.answer_grounding: Oracle answers reference in-text spans only; no external verification injected
data_discipline.oracle_format: JSONL with id, source_id, category, reference_answer, supporting_source_spans, difficulty
data_discipline.thermometer_scope: One document only; narrative mechanism probe, not real-world-transfer or native-corpus score contribution
prior_containment_questions: Does compile import remembered facts that are absent from source.md?
Does query answer from compiled source evidence rather than remembered plot?
Does the instrument preserve source-local aliases and fabricated in-story claims?
Does it respect Watson's narrator limits before Holmes's retrospective explanation?
